playerContract.fantasy: Tune a suspended cathedral mobile until three weighted bowls sing in exact balance.
playerContract.heroVerb: shift and settle a physical counterweight
loop: shift the canonical body along the rail
settle it as a canonical weighted source
overload and restart or complete exact balance
playerContract.qualityBar: Preserve the validated feel scores and the readable diagnostics-closed first screen.
requiredProof: enter and understand with Advanced closed

core-runtime-exports.scope: NexusEngine at the pinned Core baseline
core-runtime-exports.result: Core exports the engine/runtime-kit primitives consumed by both installed domains.

core-no-weight-source-bridge.scope: Pinned Core source at a5882b47 searched for physicsBodyLite, weightedTrigger, weight source, weighted source, pressure plate, mass sensor, setWeightSource, and settled body
core-no-weight-source-bridge.result: No matching Core responsibility or export exists.

protokits-physics-body-lite.result: Physics Body Lite canonically owns body registration, mass, deterministic impulse integration, velocity, friction, state, descriptors, events, and reset.

protokits-weighted-trigger.result: Weighted Trigger canonically owns trigger bounds, idempotent weight-source upsert, aggregate weight, activation, state, descriptors, events, and reset.

protokits-endpoints-without-bridge.scope: ProtoKits source and docs searched for weight/body projection, pressure plate, mass sensor, and settled-body observation
protokits-endpoints-without-bridge.result: The two canonical endpoint services exist, but no domain owns projecting an authoritative body snapshot into a weighted source.

classification-local-by-contract.result: Authored fantasy, scene/tuning, player policy, browser input, Canvas, DOM, diagnostics, and outcome presentation remain application or adapter responsibilities in Experiments.

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Install the selected domain kits into one engine lifecycle.
responsibilities.invariant: One engine owns one installed Physics Body Lite service and one installed Weighted Trigger service per session.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: A fresh session creates one fresh engine; kit installation is delegated to Core.
proof: installed APIs appear in Advanced evidence

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Advance installed deterministic domains after registration, impulse, and source updates.
responsibilities.invariant: Domain state is read only after the explicit Core tick has resolved the update.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Ticks do not install another owner.

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Own counterweight position, mass, velocity, impulse response, size, and friction.
responsibilities.invariant: The route never owns a second physical body state.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: registerBody replaces the same body id; reset restores the known empty domain state.
proof: registered bodies expose expected mass and position
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: route-local physics would duplicate the canonical domain

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Own bounded weight sources, aggregate trigger weight, activation, and release facts.
responsibilities.invariant: Each source id contributes at most once and activation derives only from canonical source positions and weights.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: registerTrigger and setWeightSource replace the same id; reset restores empty state.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: an exact-weight trigger variant would duplicate this owner

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Expose one frozen declarative application record with source, ownership, dependencies, content, and proof budgets.
responsibilities.invariant: Consumers cannot mutate the application contract or acquire reusable simulation behavior from it.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Every factory call returns the same frozen value.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: inline route constants would recreate a second application owner

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Declare the fantasy, controls, stones, bowls, rail tuning, outcomes, quality bar, and performance budget.
responsibilities.invariant: All branded scene content and proof budgets remain data in the application owner.
proof: route renders three authored stones and bowls

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Own selected stone, settled flags, terminal result, action count, and bounded player messages for one run.
responsibilities.invariant: Exactly one local session object owns application progress; reusable domains own only their own state.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Selection replaces one id; messages stay capped at four; terminal state is one-way until restart.
proof: selected/settled/result states appear in public proof
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: must not become a second physics or trigger owner

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Translate left/right hero commands into bounded, mass-scaled impulses along the authored rail.
responsibilities.invariant: Movement clamps to the authored rail and mutates only Physics Body Lite through its public API.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Edge commands are no-ops; accepted commands produce one impulse and one action increment.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: generic impulse integration already belongs to Physics Body Lite

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Project an explicitly settled canonical body snapshot into one idempotent weighted source and resolve the receiving domain.
responsibilities.invariant: Source id equals body id; position and mass come from Physics Body Lite; repeated projection replaces rather than duplicates; the adapter owns no body, trigger, outcome, input, or presentation state.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: setWeightSource replaces the existing source with the same body id.

lifecycle.reset: owned by the two endpoint domains

plausibleConsumers: a pressure-plate host driven by canonical bodies
a cargo or vehicle mass-sensor host
proof: settled sources produce expected aggregate weights
two successful replays produce byte-identical endpoint states
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: The route repeats the projection body in syncStone and settle; taxonomy must identify one canonical adapter owner before any implementation change.
responsibilities.promotionPotential: selected for taxonomy review only; not accepted or promoted

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Resolve authored rail positions and stone weights to the exact bowl definitions used by play and guidance.
responsibilities.invariant: The same frozen bowl data drives placement, prompt guidance, strip highlighting, and Canvas highlighting.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Pure lookup over frozen application data.

lifecycle.snapshot: derived from application and domain state

proof: selected stone identifies and highlights its exact bowl
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: presentation must continue consuming this one derived match

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Interpret canonical trigger weights as terminal overload failure or all-bowls-exact success.
responsibilities.invariant: Any weight above required loses immediately; success requires every bowl active and exactly equal to required weight.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Terminal result is written once and blocks later actions until restart.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: must not be added to generic Weighted Trigger

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Recover from either terminal outcome by recreating the complete session from frozen data.
responsibilities.invariant: Restart returns selection, local progress, Core engine, and both domain states to the same known initial state.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Repeated restart recreates the same initial state without retaining listeners or domain state inside the session.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: global browser listeners remain singletons outside recreated session state

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Represent exact equality as a second reusable weighted-trigger mode.
responsibilities.invariant: No second trigger owner may be created when canonical aggregate weight plus local outcome equality already covers the need.
responsibilities.sourceOwner: not implemented; represented by Weighted Trigger plus local completion policy
outputs: none; rejected as a separate owner
proof: exact success already passes with canonical aggregate state
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: would duplicate trigger state and equality policy

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Translate buttons and keyboard keys into selection, shift, settle, and restart commands.
responsibilities.invariant: Browser listeners own no simulation state and are registered once for the page lifetime.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: One listener path per control surface; key repeats are ignored.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: must not move browser handles into ProtoKits

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Project application progress, matching-bowl guidance, status, and terminal outcome into accessible DOM.
responsibilities.invariant: One DOM surface consumes canonical application and domain state without mutating either.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: render replaces generated stone and bowl content instead of appending owners.

events: click handlers on current stone buttons

responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: generated button listeners are replaced with their nodes; no second status owner exists

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Render the rail, bowls, selected target, stones, and ambient field into one Canvas.
responsibilities.invariant: One Canvas and one animation-frame owner derive visual state from the session and canonical domains.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: Each frame clears and redraws the one Canvas; resize mutates only its backing size.
responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: must remain the sole Canvas and animation-frame owner

responsibilities.semanticResponsibility: Expose default-closed domain evidence, serializable public state, bounded frame samples, and runtime metrics.
responsibilities.invariant: Diagnostics never become a second gameplay owner and remain closed on the final player surface.
responsibilities.idempotencyRule: One frozen proof API and one bounded frame-sample array exist per page.

lifecycle.reset: session state resets while page metrics remain bounded

responsibilities.duplicateOrMergeRisk: no parallel debug panel or metric owner is allowed

rejectedAlternatives.reason: Bundles authored puzzle outcome, scene, controls, and adapters around two existing reusable domains.
Names one game's sculpture rather than one reusable semantic responsibility.

taxonomyReviewCandidate.selectionReason: It is the only mapped seam with one semantic responsibility, deterministic inputs and outputs, idempotent endpoint behavior, negative Core/domain bridge evidence, and at least two plausible consumers. It is still duplicated across two local call paths, so taxonomy must decide ownership before implementation changes.
reviewQuestions: Should this remain a route-local adapter or become a small integration kit under the weighted-trigger taxonomy?
Which endpoint owns scheduling the receiving-domain tick without creating cross-domain lifecycle coupling?
Can callers consume one projection command without understanding both endpoint APIs?

currentRunParity.timingAssessment: The local automation browser was consistently compositor-capped near 30 FPS in headed, headless, and MCP sessions, so its rAF timing is not comparable to the validated 60.26 FPS baseline. The three runtime files are byte-identical to the starting commit; change-induced primary-metric regression is therefore 0%, while bounded counts, zero active-play long tasks, and zero console messages passed.

unresolvedGaps: The selected adapter boundary has not received canonical kit/subdomain ownership.
No consumer migration or successor implementation is authorized yet.
The preserved playable remains the parity owner and is not purge-eligible.
nextStageEntryGate: Use this complete 17-responsibility map as the sole intake.
Review only settled-body-weight-source-projection as the taxonomy candidate.
Choose canonical local, kit, or subdomain ownership without implementing promotion, migration, or purge.
Preserve the playable route and both canonical endpoint services.
